name: Berkay Kullukçu
summary: Acoustics researcher and mechanical engineer with experience in vehicle sound, psychoacoustics, vibro-acoustic condition monitoring, signal processing, experimental design, finite-element simulation, flexible sensors, and product-oriented R&D.

education.degree: Doctor of Engineering (Dr.-Ing.) in Electrical Engineering
education.institution: Technische Universität Dresden, Chair of Acoustics and Haptics

experience.title: Scientific Employee
experience.company: Department of Machine Dynamics and Acoustics, TH Wildau
bullets: Conduct research on acoustic and vibration-based condition monitoring of rail-vehicle components.
Investigate loose bolted joints, structural defects, tightening torque, and vibro-acoustic nonlinearities.

experience.title: Doctoral Researcher
experience.company: Chair of Acoustics and Haptics, TU Dresden
bullets: Develop and evaluate AVAS concepts for electric trucks using psychoacoustic testing and semantic sound evaluation.
Design listening experiments for comfort, annoyance, urgency, masking, and perceptual discrimination.
Use binaural synthesis, signal analysis, statistics, and psychoacoustic metrics to translate perception into design guidance.

experience.title: Design Engineer
bullets: Worked on CT7/T700 turboshaft and turboprop engine design.

experience.title: Co-Founder
bullets: Developed hospital-digitalization and IoT monitoring concepts for ventilation and infusion equipment.
Worked on integrated-circuit and device-communication solutions; contributed to patented medical-device systems.

experience.title: Flexible Electronics Researcher
bullets: Worked in thin films and flexible electronic sensors.
Developed LabVIEW software for experimental testing.

experience.title: Researcher
bullets: Researched flexible polymer energy harvesters, PMUTs, micro/nanofabrication, and acoustic equivalent-circuit models.
Developed a wearable sensor-system concept for venous dislodgement detection.

publications.title: Investigating perceptual discrimination thresholds for attributes of whole-body vibration
publications.authors: B. Kullukçu, J. Krautwurm, S. Merchel, R. Rosenkranz, M. E. Altinsoy

publications.title: Electric Truck Sound Design: A User-Centered Psychoacoustic Approach to AVAS
publications.authors: B. Kullukçu, R. Rosenkranz, M. E. Altinsoy
publications.venue: Journal of the Audio Engineering Society

publications.title: Piezoelectric PVDF-TrFE/PET Energy Harvesters for Structural Health Monitoring Applications
Design and Modeling of a PVDF-TrFE Flexible Wind Energy Harvester
publications.venue: Turkish Journal of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences

publications.title: Psychoacoustic Modelling of AVAS Sounds: Consumer-Centric Semantic Attribute Development for Electric Vehicles
publications.authors: B. Kullukçu, M. Engel, S. Atamer, M. E. Altinsoy

publications.title: Experimental Determination of the Masking Threshold in Electric Trucks
publications.authors: B. Kullukçu, O. Zeidler, S. Atamer, M. E. Altinsoy

projects.name: Electric-Truck AVAS Research
bullets: User-centered sound design, semantic profiling, listening tests, psychoacoustic metrics, and binaural synthesis.
Published in JAES and presented across DAGA, INTER-NOISE, Forum Acusticum, and ICSV.

projects.name: Bolted-Joint Vibro-Acoustic Diagnostics
bullets: Experimental characterization of bolted joints under varying tightening torque.
Frequency-modulated and single-tone excitation for detecting nonlinear behavior and looseness.

projects.name: Flexible Piezoelectric Energy Harvesters
bullets: PVDF-TrFE/PET energy harvesting for structural-health monitoring.
Combined analytical modeling, FEM, fabrication, and experimental validation.

projects.name: Patented Medical Monitoring Systems
bullets: Co-inventor on three Turkish patents/utility models covering infusion-pump detection, invasive devices, and a nasogastric-tube system.

awards.name: Graduate Academy Conference Travel Grant - DAGA

courses.name: Interdisciplinary Module
courses.description: Teaching support for a senior engineering class.

courses.name: Sound Design
courses.description: Supervision of senior engineering students in vehicle sound-design projects.

courses.name: Acoustic Hands-on Experimenting
courses.description: Supervision of household-appliance sound-power measurement projects.

courses.name: Master-level Research Supervision
courses.description: Supervised a six-month vehicle-acoustics research internship; final grade 1.0.

courses.name: Mechanical Engineering Laboratory
courses.description: Laboratory tutoring for senior mechanical-engineering students.

internships.title: Automotive Engineering Intern - R&D
bullets: Developed software exceeding 20,000 lines of code to help R&D engineers manipulate CATIA 3D parts.

internships.title: Mechanical Engineering Intern - Safety Management Systems
bullets: Supported engineering work in safety management systems.

internships.title: Mechanical Engineering Intern - R&D
bullets: Performed Patran FEM and 3D CAD work for aerospace component design.
